notes: Source-controlled internal card tag taxonomy. Role tags describe deck construction roles. Salt tags describe drivers of resentment, frustration, or Rule 0 concern. Social-friction tags describe table-experience patterns separate from raw power. Salt and social-friction weights are 0-100 scale contributions to Pod Tracker's deterministic salt and social-friction score components.

tags.label: Ramp
tags.description: Cards that accelerate mana via lands, mana rocks, or mana dorks beyond a normal land drop.

tags.label: Fast mana
tags.description: Mana acceleration that produces more than one mana for one mana of investment, or accelerates ahead of a normal turn one curve.

tags.label: Tutor
tags.description: Cards that search the library for a specific card or narrow card pool.

tags.label: Card draw
tags.description: Recurring or burst card draw, card selection, and engines that refill the hand.

tags.label: Protection
tags.description: Effects that protect the controller, the commander, the board, or a key combo piece.

tags.label: Removal
tags.description: Targeted removal for creatures, planeswalkers, artifacts, enchantments, or other permanents.

tags.label: Stack interaction
tags.description: Counterspells and stack-based interaction that answers spells before they resolve.

tags.label: Board wipe
tags.description: Mass removal of creatures, permanents, or other broad board states.

tags.label: Stax
tags.description: Resource-denial pieces that constrain mana, untaps, casting, or symmetric play.

tags.label: Combo piece
tags.description: Cards that participate in compact two-card or three-card win combos or infinite loops.

tags.label: Graveyard use
tags.description: Cards that fill, mine, or value the graveyard as a resource.

tags.label: Land
tags.description: Lands and land-equivalent permanents that produce mana or fix colors.

tags.label: Win condition
tags.description: Cards that close the game directly or anchor a primary win line.

tags.label: Salt driver: fast mana
tags.description: Fast mana that creates a power gap on early turns and pulls casual tables out of band.

tags.label: Salt driver: mass land denial
tags.description: Effects that destroy or lock down all lands, forcing opponents to recover from zero mana.

tags.label: Salt driver: extra turns
tags.description: Extra-turn spells, especially when chained or recurred, that compress opponent agency.

tags.label: Salt driver: chaos
tags.description: Random or coin-flip effects that disconnect outcome from decision quality.

tags.label: Salt driver: theft and control
tags.description: Effects that steal opponents' cards, permanents, or turns rather than answer them.

tags.label: Salt driver: repetitive loop
tags.description: Loops or recursion patterns that produce repetitive game states without advancing toward a win.

tags.label: Salt driver: stax lockpiece
tags.description: Soft- or hard-lock stax pieces that disproportionately punish opponents while the controller breaks symmetry.

tags.label: Salt driver: compact combo
tags.description: Two-card or commander-enabled win combos that close games before opponents have a meaningful response window.

tags.label: Friction: combo opacity
tags.description: Win lines that are hard for opponents to recognize or interact with from public information.

tags.label: Friction: long-game pressure
tags.description: Game plans that extend match length or stall out boards without clear closing pressure.

tags.label: Friction: disclosure required
tags.description: Cards or strategies that require Rule 0 disclosure for fair table expectations.

tags.label: Friction: interaction asymmetry
tags.description: Pod-impacting interaction patterns that fall heavily on one player or one archetype.
